primary_sources.title: RFC 8032: Edwards-Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (EdDSA)
primary_sources.relevance: Defines Ed25519 signing and verification semantics used to bind a retained ledger checkpoint to a separately trusted public key.

primary_sources.title: RFC 8785: JSON Canonicalization Scheme
primary_sources.relevance: Motivates invariant JSON bytes before signing; the prototype implements a constrained deterministic canonicalizer for the checkpoint schema.

primary_sources.title: R-041 preregistered acoustic trial ledger
primary_sources.relevance: R-041 detects mutation when an earlier trusted digest is retained but explicitly cannot stop a controller from rewriting the full ledger and recomputing its chain.

finding: A hash chain needs an independently trusted checkpoint. Signing the protocol hash, current head hash, ledger identity, trial count, and issuance time with Ed25519 converts whole-ledger rewrite from an undetectable local recomputation into a signature or trust-policy failure, provided the verifier retains the trusted public key and expected state outside the ledger controller's rewrite boundary.
design_implication: After each preregistered milestone, sign the R-041 ledger head with createSignedCheckpoint and persist the signed checkpoint plus trusted public-key mapping in a separate evidence boundary. Before statistical evaluation, require both verifyTrialLedger and verifySignedCheckpoint to accept the same protocol hash, head hash, and trial count.

verification.route: isolated Node test run using the committed logic reproduced byte-for-byte for the changed key-handling path
covered: complete rewrite under untrusted key-id rejection

uncertainty: The checkpoint is only as trustworthy as the separately managed trusted public-key mapping and retained expected state.
A compromised trusted private key can sign a rewritten ledger; this prototype does not provide hardware-backed keys, transparency-log inclusion, revocation, or trusted timestamps.
The constrained canonicalizer is not claimed as a complete general-purpose RFC 8785 implementation.
Cryptographic integrity does not prove trial independence, correct experimental execution, physical audibility, or causal interpretation.
falsification_route: Demonstrate a changed protocol hash, head hash, trial count, ledger identity, signature, or key-id that is accepted under an unchanged trusted-key map and expected state.
Demonstrate a full rewrite signed by an untrusted key that is accepted.
Demonstrate two checkpoint objects valid under this constrained schema that have the same semantics but produce different canonical bytes.
Demonstrate a valid unchanged trusted checkpoint that the verifier rejects.
